Title: Reduced RG-II pectin dimerization disrupts differential growth by attenuating hormonal regulation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN3 plasma membrane abundance and transcript levels are reduced in mur1 and restored by boron
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PIN3-GFP imaging; fluorescence quantification; qRT-PCR; boron rescue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: apical hook plasma membrane in dark-grown seedlings
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN3 abundance at the plasma membrane is reduced in mur1. 2. PIN3 transcript abundance is reduced in mur1 and restored by boron. 3. PIN3 is a downstream component of the ARF7/ARF19-dependent auxin transport defect caused by reduced RG-II dimerization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carrier, not a biosynthetic enzyme or TF; categorized as Common Gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating PIN3 may alter differential growth responses but likely affects multiple developmental processes.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Establishes auxin asymmetry to repress inner-side growth during hook development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIN4 plasma membrane abundance and transcript levels are reduced in mur1 and restored by boron
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PIN4-GFP imaging; fluorescence quantification; qRT-PCR; boron rescue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: apical hook plasma membrane in dark-grown seedlings
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN4 abundance at the plasma membrane is reduced in mur1. 2. PIN4 transcript levels are reduced in mur1 and restored by boron. 3. PIN4 is part of the disrupted polar auxin transport machinery downstream of RG-II dimerization defects.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carrier, not a pathway enzyme or TF; categorized as Common Gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting PIN4 may modify auxin patterning but may cause pleiotropy.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes auxin accumulation/asymmetry required for hook development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AUX1 plasma membrane abundance and transcript levels are reduced in mur1 and restored by boron
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: AUX1-YFP imaging; fluorescence quantification; qRT-PCR; boron rescue
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: apical hook plasma membrane in dark-grown seedlings
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AUX1 abundance at the plasma membrane is reduced in mur1. 2. AUX1 transcript abundance is reduced in mur1 and restored by boron. 3. AUX1 contributes to the auxin transport defect linked to reduced RG-II dimerization.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carrier, not a pathway enzyme or TF; categorized as Common Gene.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to tune auxin-dependent growth responses, with likely broad developmental consequences.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: GDP-L-fucose biosynthesis; RG-II pectin side-chain biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: de novo GDP-L-fucose synthesis supplying RG-II fucosylation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; first committed step in de novo GDP-L-fucose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GDP-D-mannose to GDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-D-mannose in the first step of de novo GDP-L-fucose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool required for RG-II side chain fucosylation and proper boron-mediated RG-II dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell wall of hypocotyl/apical hook tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports cell wall integrity, differential growth, and proper apical hook development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MUR1 reduces RG-II dimerization and disrupts apical hook development; fucose or boron supplementation suppresses the defect
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis of mur1-1 and mur1-2; chemical rescue with L-fucose and boric acid; RG-II dimerization-related phenocopy with PBA; kinematic hook analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-grown seedlings; boric acid supplementation; phenylboronic acid treatment; exogenous L-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream control step for GDP-L-fucose supply to RG-II dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering GDP-L-fucose supply may improve cell wall integrity-dependent growth traits, but broad developmental effects warrant caution
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe growth and morphogenesis defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MUR1 is required for proper RG-II pectin dimerization in Arabidopsis seedlings. 2. mur1 mutants show reduced hook angle, faster hook opening, and disrupted differential growth. 3. Boron or exogenous fucose suppresses mur1 defects, linking GDP-L-fucose supply to RG-II-dependent growth control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper experimentally distinguishes MUR1-mediated RG-II effects from xyloglucan fucosylation by showing mur2 mutants lack hook defects.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-22 hydroxylation reactions in brassinosteroid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms biosynthetic intermediates required for brassinosteroid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes growth and contributes to apical hook development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DWF4 transcript is reduced in mur1 and restored by boron, implicating reduced BR biosynthesis in hook defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in WT and mur1 with/without boron; physiological rescue of mur1 by exogenous EBR and bes1-D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: apical hooks of 2-day-old dark-grown seedlings assayed
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key BR biosynthetic control step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating DWF4 may tune BR-dependent growth responses, but likely causes pleiotropic architecture effects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered BR biosynthesis can broadly affect plant stature and development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DWF4 expression is down-regulated in mur1 seedlings. 2. Boron restoration of RG-II dimerization restores DWF4 transcript levels. 3. Reduced DWF4 expression is associated with reduced BR pathway output contributing to mur1 hook defects.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DWF4 is treated here mainly as a downstream biosynthetic readout rather than being directly genetically manipulated.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation reactions in brassinosteroid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms biosynthetic intermediates required for brassinosteroid production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes growth and contributes to apical hook development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ROT3 transcript is reduced in mur1 and restored by boron, linking RG-II dimerization to BR biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in WT and mur1 with/without boron; physiological rescue of mur1 by exogenous EBR and bes1-D
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: apical hooks of 2-day-old dark-grown seedlings assayed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can serve as a BR-biosynthetic engineering target, though this paper uses it as pathway output marker
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of BR biosynthesis may alter overall growth and morphology
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ROT3 expression is reduced in mur1 seedlings. 2. Boron treatment restores ROT3 transcript abundance. 3. Reduced ROT3 expression supports the conclusion that BR biosynthesis is attenuated in mur1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like DWF4, ROT3 is a downstream biosynthetic gene used to support BR pathway attenuation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor (ARF) family; B3 DNA-binding domain and Aux/IAA interaction PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of auxin transport and brassinosteroid biosynthesis-related genes affecting hormone-mediated differential growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates or promotes expression of PIN3, PIN4, AUX1 and positively contributes to DWF4 and ROT3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cell wall perturbation from reduced RG-II dimerization; auxin pathway context; boron-mediated restoration of RG-II dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: links auxin pathway to BR biosynthesis, suggesting feedforward control toward BR pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key downstream regulator connecting RG-II cell wall status to auxin maxima and BR biosynthetic gene expression during hook development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ARF7 expression in mur1 contributes to auxin transport defects and hook defects; epidermal ARF7 expression fully suppresses mur1 hook phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; arf7 arf19 mutant transcript analysis for PIN3/PIN4/AUX1 and DWF4/ROT3; transgenic rescue using proML1::ARF7-mCherry in mur1; hook kinematics
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: measured in apical hooks of dark-grown seedlings; epidermal expression used for rescue
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely functions with ARF19 in redundant auxin-response regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF7 transcript abundance is significantly reduced in mur1 and restored by boron. 2. Reduced ARF7/ARF19 activity is associated with lower expression of auxin transport genes and BR biosynthetic genes. 3. Epidermis-specific ARF7 overexpression completely suppresses mur1 hook defects.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ARF7 is one of the most directly validated core regulators in the paper because its transgenic re-expression rescues mur1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Tissue-specific enhancement of ARF7 could compensate for growth defects caused by cell wall impairment, but broad auxin-related pleiotropy should be considered.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor (ARF) family; B3 DNA-binding domain and PB1 domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional regulation of auxin transport and brassinosteroid biosynthesis-related genes affecting differential growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively contributes to expression of auxin transport components and BR biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cell wall perturbation from reduced RG-II dimerization; boron-mediated restoration
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of auxin-to-BR feedforward regulation during hook development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: redundant key regulator with ARF7 required for proper auxin response maximum and BR biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ARF19 expression in mur1 contributes to hook defects through reduced auxin transport gene and BR biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; arf7 arf19 mutant transcript analysis; phenotypic comparison to mur1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: measured in apical hooks of dark-grown seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF19 transcript levels are significantly reduced in mur1 and restored by boron. 2. Loss of ARF7/ARF19 reduces PIN3, PIN4, AUX1, DWF4, and ROT3 expression. 3. ARF19 acts with ARF7 as a central downstream regulatory module linking RG-II dimerization status to hormone pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike ARF7, direct transgenic rescue was not reported specifically for ARF19 alone.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating ARF19 together with ARF7 may tune cell-wall-responsive growth programs but may cause auxin-related pleiotropy.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: brassinosteroid signaling output controlling growth responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: activation by dephosphorylation and nuclear accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: constitutively active bes1-D enhances BR response and suppresses mur1 hook defects
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: brassinosteroid signaling; boron-enhanced RG-II dimerization increases dephosphorylated BES1 fraction
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in BR feedback onto RG-II dimerization indirectly through BR action
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key BR signaling effector whose activated form bypasses BR attenuation in mur1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive BES1 signaling suppresses mur1 hook defects; boron increases dephosphorylated BES1, indicating enhanced BR signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic suppression using bes1-D in mur1; immunoblot analysis of phosphorylated/dephosphorylated BES1 ratios
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark-grown seedlings; boric acid supplementation; EBR treatment context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bes1-D genetically suppresses mur1 hook defects. 2. Boron increases the dephosphorylated/total BES1 ratio, indicating enhanced BR signaling. 3. BES1 functions downstream of BR pathway attenuation associated with reduced RG-II dimerization.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study indicates BR signaling per se is not reduced in mur1 under basal conditions, but can be enhanced by boron-mediated RG-II dimerization.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive BR signaling may produce broad growth alterations
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Activated BES1 signaling could compensate for cell-wall-associated growth defects, but constitutive BR response likely has strong pleiotropic effects.
